author: oscarhenrycollins

manifest.author: Humanity Labs
manifest.description: Chat with your OpenClaw AI agent in a sidebar. Your vault is the agent's workspace.
manifest.name: OpenClaw

name: OpenClaw

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
## What's new

ObsidianClaw now tells users about a required one-time OpenClaw patch — the gateway rejects Obsidian's `app://obsidian.md` origin out of the box, and this is the first release where the requirement is documented and surfaced in onboarding.

### Changes

- **README**: new "One-time OpenClaw patch (required)" section with a single-command installer (`curl … | sudo bash`).
- **Onboarding**: orange callout on the welcome screen explains the requirement and offers a copy-paste command.
- **Connect failures**: after 3 reasonless handshake closes in a row, a notice nudges the user to re-apply the patch (most often needed right after an `openclaw update`).
- **New script**: `scripts/patch-openclaw.sh` — idempotent, backs up before editing, fails closed if OpenClaw's source layout shifts.

### Why this is needed

Obsidian's renderer loads from `app://obsidian.md`. Per the URL spec, custom schemes get a "null" origin, so adding `app://obsidian.md` to OpenClaw's allowlist doesn't match. The patch adds one fallback that compares the raw origin string. We're tracking an upstream fix so the requirement can be removed in a future release.

### Upgrading

Drop-in. No setting changes. Re-run the patch script after every `openclaw update`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/oscarhenrycollins/obsidianclaw/compare/0.41.10...0.41.11

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What changed

- Removes the top-right agent switcher from the chat header; agent switching now lives in the control panel.
- Renames the verbose control to ClawTabs-style “show steps”.
- Changes the attachment button from paperclip to plus.
- Restyles the control panel to more closely match the ClawTabs dashboard: compact agent identity, flat section rows, group labels, and a slimmer left drawer.

## Validation

- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm run build
- GitHub release workflow passed with artifact attestation

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What changed

- Adds a compact ClawTabs-style control panel drawer inside Obsidian.
- Moves the mobile tab hamburger to the right side and adds a left-side control panel button.
- Refreshes mobile tab switcher styling to more closely match ClawTabs touch targets and layout.
- Improves pairing/onboarding messaging so pending approval is shown as an approval step instead of a generic gateway failure.

## Validation

- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm run build
- GitHub release workflow passed with artifact attestation

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What changed

- Updates the Obsidian plugin handshake to OpenClaw protocol 4 for current gateway compatibility.
- Refreshes README/install instructions now that OpenClaw is live in the Obsidian Community Plugin directory.
- Keeps release assets built through GitHub Actions with artifact attestations for main.js and styles.css.

## Validation

- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm run build
- GitHub artifact attestation for main.js and styles.css

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Fix: show default values in bar chips + sync settings with onboarding

- Bar chips now show inherited defaults: 'think: default (high)', 'verbose: default (on)'
- Loads defaults from gateway config on connect/reconnect
- Settings tab and onboarding wizard now sync with the chat view
- No more manually resetting session key in settings after setup wizard

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Add thinking level and verbose mode bar control chips.

- Clickable chips next to model pill: think + verbose
- Think cycles: default → off → low → medium → high
- Verbose cycles: default → off → on → full
- Shows inherited defaults, highlights overrides
- Syncs with gateway via sessions.patch

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Fix all ObsidianReviewBot required lint issues for community plugin approval.

- Replace all innerHTML with DOM-based SVG creation
- Replace all inline styles with CSS classes + custom properties
- Remove unnecessary type assertions, unused variables
- ESLint: 0 errors, 0 warnings

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Fix: channel session close button**
- Telegram/Discord/etc. sessions can't be deleted (gateway protects them)
- × on channel sessions now resets the conversation instead
- Plugin-created tabs (numbered tabs) still delete normally
- Notice shows 'Reset: name' so it's clear what happened

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Debug: detailed console logging for session delete to diagnose Telegram session issue

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Fixes:**
- Session delete now tries both key formats (fixes Telegram/channel sessions not deleting)
- Removed sub-agent tabs (gateway doesn't reliably clean them up; will revisit when lifecycle events exist)
- Tabs still auto-refresh when sessions are added/removed externally

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Fixes:**
- Sub-agent tabs: just 🤖 emoji (no truncated text)
- Sub-agent tabs: only visible while actively running, auto-hide when idle >2min

- Channel sessions (Telegram, etc.): × properly deletes the session
- Completed sub-agents no longer linger as ghost tabs

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Fixes:**
- Channel session tabs (Telegram, Discord, etc.): × now resets instead of trying to delete (channels recreate their session, making delete look broken)
- Sub-agent tabs now auto-appear when spawned (detected via session list polling)
- Sub-agent tabs auto-disappear when completed
- Sub-agent tabs hide the × button (they self-close)
- Tabs auto-refresh whenever the session list changes

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Sub-agent tabs (view-only)**

- Input area disabled with banner: 'Sub-agent running in the background. This tab will close when completed.'
- Tabs auto-close when the sub-agent finishes
- Auto-switches back to Main if you're viewing a completed sub-agent
- Zero performance overhead: piggybacks on existing session polling

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:

- ↻ reset button clears the current tab's conversation

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: All models always clickable (no more stuck selection), delete refreshes from gateway, disable voice recording to prevent context bloat
releases.name: v0.18.3

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Provider-first model picker (browse by provider → pick model), fix session delete persistence, fix context meter for non-main sessions
releases.name: v0.18.2

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Fix model picker duplicate selection; add chat naming for new chat button
releases.name: v0.18.1

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Layout redesign:**

Context bar: `[Main ▾]  [═══░░░] 42%  [Compact]`
Input row: `[🧠] [📎] [message...] [send]`

**What changed:**
- 🧠 **Brain icon** next to the attach button opens the model picker
- Context bar is now purely about your current **chat** — no model clutter
- Model picker shows name + provider only (no context sizes)

**Friendly naming:**
- "Agent" → **"Assistant"** everywhere
- "Session" → **"Chat"** everywhere
- Cryptic keys get friendly names:
  - `main` → "Main"
  - `telegram:slash:123` → "Telegram"
  - Gateway `displayName` used when available

**Chat picker** (click Main ▾):
- "Assistant: main" header + ⚙ settings shortcut
- "Want more assistants?" with docs link
- Delete chats with ✕ button
- "+ New chat" at bottom

**Model picker** (click 🧠):
- Clean list: name + provider
- "Want more models? Add them in your gateway config."
releases.name: 0.18.0 — Brain Icon + Friendly Names

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Two separate pickers, each focused on one thing:

**Context bar:** `[main ▾]  [═══░░░] 42%  [opus-4-6 ▾]  [Compact]`

**Session picker** (click session pill):
- Agent name at top + ⚙ cog opens plugin settings
- "Want more agents? Configure on your gateway" with docs link
- Session list: context meter + % per session
- **Delete sessions** — ✕ button on non-current sessions
- `+ New session` at bottom

**Model picker** (click model pill):
- All models with provider + context window size
- Click to switch instantly
- "Want more models? Configure on your gateway" footer with docs link

Session delete uses the `sessions.delete` gateway RPC.
releases.name: 0.17.0 — Session & Model Pickers

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Clean separation of concerns:

**Context bar:** `[main / opus-4-6 ▾]  [═══░░░] 42%  [Compact]`

Click the pill → unified switcher with two sections:

**SESSION** — all active sessions listed
- Current session highlighted (● dot + accent border)
- Click any other session to switch instantly
- `+ New session` at the bottom

**MODEL** — all available models, scrollable
- Current model highlighted
- Click to switch (one click, done)
- Shows provider + context window size

**Compact** stays on the context bar — one click, no modal.

Server-side config (gateway, agents, Tailscale, pairing) lives in Settings + Setup Wizard.
Client-side switching (session, model) lives in the context bar pill.
releases.name: 0.16.0 — Unified Switcher

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Context bar now shows the full hierarchy:

`[session ▾]  [═══░░░] 42%  [model]  [Compact]`

**Session pill** — click to open session manager:
- Clickable session rows (one click to switch)
- Current session highlighted with accent border
- Mini context meters per session
- `+ New session` row at the bottom

**Model pill** — click to open model picker:
- All available models from your gateway
- Shows provider, context window size, reasoning support
- One click to switch (no confirmation needed)

**Compact** — right there on the context bar, one click

Agent switching supported when multiple agents are configured.
releases.name: 0.15.0 — Context Bar Redesign

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
New session pill in context bar + redesigned session manager:

**Context bar:**
- Clickable session pill replaces gear button — shows current session name + dropdown arrow
- Click to open session manager

**Session Manager (redesigned):**
- **Agent tier** — pill selector for switching between agents (when multiple are configured)
- **Session tier** — all sessions for the selected agent:
  - Current session highlighted with accent border
  - Mini context meter per session
  - One-click switch between sessions
- **Actions** — Compact (📦) and New Session (✨)
- **Hierarchy footer** — Gateway > Agent > Session > Model

Supports `agents.list` RPC for multi-agent setups. Single-agent gateways just show the session list.
releases.name: 0.14.0 — Multi-Agent Session Manager

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Unified session management:

- Context bar: Compact + New buttons replaced with single ⚙ gear button
- Session Manager modal shows:
  - Current session (model, context % used)
  - **Compact context** — summarize chat to free space
  - **Start fresh** — archive and reset (with confirmation dialog)
  - **Session switcher** — see all sessions, one-click switch
- Cron/internal sessions hidden from the list
- Foundation for multi-agent switching (coming next)
releases.name: 0.13.0 — Session Manager

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Settings page redesign:
- Setup wizard is now the hero element at the top
- Connection status dot (green/red) with gateway URL
- Session key in its own section
- Connection fields labeled as advanced ('set by wizard')
- Numbered steps in gateway setup wizard step

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Complete rewrite of the setup wizard — 6 clear steps that walk you through everything:

1. **Install** — Confirm OpenClaw is running
2. **Network** — Install Tailscale on both devices
3. **Gateway** — Set up Tailscale Serve (copy-paste commands)
4. **Connect** — Paste URL + token, test connection
5. **Pair** — Approve device with exact terminal commands
6. **Done** — Tips for sidebar chat, Control UI, and Obsidian Sync

No more gateway config confusion. Tailscale Serve is the one recommended method. Every step has copy-paste commands. URL auto-normalizes (paste https:// or wss://, both work).
releases.name: 0.12.0 — Onboarding Overhaul

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Tailscale Serve as primary gateway connection method

- Setup wizard now guides to wss:// Tailscale Serve URLs (not raw IPs)
- Auto-normalizes URLs: paste https://, http://, wss://, or ws:// — all work
- Updated troubleshooting with tailscale serve setup commands
- No more fighting loopback vs lan vs tailnet bind configs

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Voice messaging (send)**
- Mic button replaces send button when input is empty (Telegram-style)
- Click mic → record, click stop → send voice to agent
- Red pulsing animation while recording
- Agent receives audio, transcribes server-side
- Uses MediaRecorder API (opus/webm codec)

**Voice messaging (receive)** 
- ▶ inline audio player in chat bubbles
- Served via gateway HTTP (works on any device)
releases.name: v0.11.0

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Audio delivered via gateway HTTP server. Agent copies audio to gateway static files, plugin fetches via HTTP URL built from the gateway connection URL. Works on any device on the Tailscale network.
releases.name: v0.10.7

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Use .txt extension for base64 voice files — gateway only allows whitelisted file types.
releases.name: v0.10.6

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Fix: add agentId:'main' to agents.files.get params
releases.name: v0.10.5

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Major rewrite: audio delivered via gateway WebSocket (agents.files.get).
- Agent saves base64-encoded audio to workspace as .b64 file
- Includes VOICE:filename.b64 ref in message text (tiny, no context bloat)
- Plugin fetches .b64 via WebSocket, decodes to Blob URL, plays
- Works on ANY device — no local filesystem needed
- Removed all deprecated MEDIA:/vault/fs approaches
releases.name: v0.10.4

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Audio reads from vault via adapter.readBinary → Blob URL. Works on all devices via Obsidian Sync.
releases.name: v0.10.3

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Read audio with require('fs') → Blob URL. Detailed console logging at every step.
releases.name: v0.10.2

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Try app://local and file:// protocols for audio. No vault copy needed.
releases.name: v0.10.1

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Fix audio playback:
- Saves voice files to openclaw-attachments/ (same folder as screenshots)
- Fixed Buffer → ArrayBuffer conversion for writeBinary
- Uses Obsidian getResourcePath (same method as image attachments)
- No more separate voice/ subfolder
releases.name: v0.10.0

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Fix audio playback: copies TTS file into vault (openclaw-attachments/voice/), then uses Obsidian's getResourcePath for a playable URL. Falls back to file:// URL if needed.
releases.name: v0.9.9

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Debug logging for TTS audio extraction
releases.name: v0.9.8

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Fix: extractContent now parses tool_result content blocks from gateway history. This is where TTS MEDIA: paths live — they were being silently dropped, so audio players never appeared.
releases.name: v0.9.7

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Fix: TTS audio players now actually appear**

The MEDIA: path lives in the tool result (a separate message), not in the assistant text. Fixed by:
- Collecting audio paths during streaming from TTS tool result events
- Attaching them to the last assistant message on stream finish
- For history reload: scanning tool_result user messages for MEDIA: paths and moving them to the preceding assistant bubble
- Audio players render with ▶ play/pause button + progress bar
releases.name: v0.9.6

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**Voice messages now play inline in Obsidian**

- TTS voice messages appear as ▶ Voice message buttons in chat bubbles
- Click to play/pause with progress bar
- Reads audio files locally via Node.js fs (works when Obsidian is on the same machine as the gateway)
- Strips raw MEDIA: paths and [[audio_as_voice]] directives from displayed text
- Supports .opus, .mp3, .mp4, .wav, .ogg, .m4a

**Voice:** Switched to George (British male, free tier premade voice)
releases.name: v0.9.5

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
**TTS audio playback in Obsidian**

When Oscar reads something aloud via the `tts` tool, the audio now plays automatically in Obsidian (on the local machine). Works for both active streams and passive watching.

- Reads audio file path from `payload.data.result.details.audioPath`
- Uses Node.js `fs` + Web Audio API — silently skipped on remote devices (they get it via Telegram)
- Handles .opus, .mp3, .mp4, .wav, .ogg

**Voice:** Sigma Centauri (futuristic AI system voice, ElevenLabs)
releases.name: v0.9.4

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
Fix auto-compaction banner: now shows the same blinking 'Compacting context...' indicator in Obsidian when the server auto-compacts context, not just when manually pressing the Compact button.

**Fixes:**
- Correctly handles phase=start (show banner) vs phase=end (hide after 2s)
- Passive device (watching another session): banner now properly hides when the stream finishes (was previously sticking forever)
- Active stream: avoids duplicate 'Compacting memory' tool entries on phase=end
releases.name: v0.9.3

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Setup wizard: copy-paste commands, troubleshooting steps on connection failure

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### New
- **Context meter** — shows how full the AI's memory is (green/yellow/red)
- **Compact button** — summarize and free space without losing context
- **New Session** — fresh start for new topics
- Streaming: tool calls interleave with text during streaming
- Info tooltip explains context, compact, and new session

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Single bubble streaming (no freeze). Tool split points snap to paragraph breaks.

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Reverted streaming to v0.4.3 behavior. Tool split points now snap to nearest line break instead of cutting mid-word.
releases.name: v0.5.2

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Text stays in one bubble during streaming. Tool calls show in the typing indicator. Final render does proper interleaving.
releases.name: v0.5.1 — Clean streaming

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### Changes
- **Image previews in chat history** — see thumbnails of images you sent
- **Send images without text** — just attach and hit send
- **Tool call interleaving persists** — tools stay between text segments after stream ends
- Text from authoritative history, tools from stream capture, combined on render
releases.name: v0.5.0

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Tool calls now persist in message history after the stream ends. Text remains clean from authoritative history.
releases.name: v0.4.4 — Persistent tool calls

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Tool calls now render in the correct position, matching the web UI. Uses the authoritative content blocks from chat history instead of reconstructing from stream events. No more text duplication or cutoff.
releases.name: v0.4.3 — Correct tool interleaving

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Fix text duplication — uses split-point tracking instead of text accumulation. Old corrupt stream data cleared.
releases.name: v0.4.2

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### Fixes
- **Message duplication gone** — text no longer repeats after tool calls. Root cause: stream text segments overlapped because they captured the full accumulated text instead of just the new portion.
- **Text cutoff fixed** — messages without tool calls now render from history (authoritative), not from stream snapshots that could be slightly behind.
- **Attachment preview thumbnails restored** — image chips show under the input before sending.

### Update
BRAT auto-updates. Force-quit Obsidian and reopen.
releases.name: v0.4.1 — Fix duplication, cutoff, previews

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### What changed
- **Images upload directly to the gateway** — no more context window explosion
- **No Obsidian Sync dependency** for images — files go over WebSocket to the Mac mini
- **Auto-resize** — images scaled to max 2048px JPEG before sending
- Gateway saves the file to disk, agent only sees the file path (~50 tokens vs 500,000)

### How it works now
1. You attach/paste an image
2. Plugin resizes it and sends via the `attachments` field in `chat.send`
3. Gateway saves to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/`
4. Agent sees `[media attached: /path/to/file.jpg]` and can view it when needed

### Install/Update
BRAT auto-updates. Force-quit Obsidian and reopen to load v0.4.0.
releases.name: v0.4.0 — Image Attachments Fixed

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### Changes
- **Tailscale-only setup** — Simplified wizard and docs for Tailscale connections only
- **BRAT installation** — Recommended install method while pending community plugin approval
- **Improved setup wizard** — Clearer instructions for auth token, Tailscale IP, and device pairing
- **Build fix** — Copies to correct `openclaw/` plugin folder

### Installation
1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) from Community Plugins
2. BRAT Settings → Add Beta Plugin → `oscarhenrycollins/obsidianclaw`
3. Works on desktop and mobile
releases.name: v0.3.1

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### What's New

- **Interleaved tool calls** — Tool calls and text now render in correct order, both during streaming and after reload. No more grouping at the end.
- **Compaction banner** — Pulsing status bar when the agent is compacting context, so you know why it's paused.
- **Animated tool indicators** — Pulsing dots on active tool calls while they're running.
- **Cleaner logs** — Removed debug logging for production.

### Links
- [obsidianclaw.ai](https://obsidianclaw.ai)
- [Bot Setup Guide](https://botsetupguide.com)
releases.name: v0.3.0

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### New
- **Rich tool labels**: See file names, command text, search queries, and domain names instead of generic "Running command" or "Fetching page"
- **Clickable URLs**: Web fetch tool calls link to the fetched URL
- **Cross-device persistence**: Tool calls and intermediary text sync across devices via Obsidian Sync
- **Smarter scrolling**: Auto-scroll for tool calls and indicators, but streaming text doesn't force-scroll — read from the top while the response continues

### Multi-Device Note
Tool call events stream live on the device you send from. Other devices see the final result after a quick restart (force-quit + reopen). Chat text streams on all devices in real-time.

### Fixes
- Passive devices now refresh on chat.final to pick up synced stream items
- Shared rendering for stream items (DRY refactor)
releases.name: 0.2.2 - Rich Tool Labels & Cross-Device Sync

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### Fixes
- Tool call indicators (🌐 Fetching page, ✏️ Writing file, etc.) now persist after response completes
- Intermediary text collapses persist after stream finishes
- Fixed stale compaction events wiping the chat DOM
- Ordered stream items re-rendered correctly after history reload

### What it looks like
Tool calls and intermediary text sections remain visible in the chat history, giving full visibility into what the agent did during a response.
releases.name: 0.2.1 - Persistent Tool Calls & Stream Items

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description:
### What's New
- **Streaming responses**: Text appears progressively as the AI generates it
- **Status indicators**: See what the agent is doing (Thinking, Reading file, Running command, Searching, Compacting memory)
- **Disconnected state UI**: Clear visual feedback when not connected, with quick access to settings
- **Abort button**: Cancel generation mid-stream

### Fixes
- Fixed content block extraction for gateway delta events
- Fixed extractText → extractContent rename that caused crash on incoming messages
releases.name: 0.2.0 - Streaming & Status Indicators

releases.author: oscarhenrycollins
releases.description: Initial release of OpenClaw for Obsidian.
